<insights>

- Isolated files — no import edges between 3 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
- Zero cohesion and zero coupling are structurally correct for a pure static-asset zone; these files have no import graph edges by definition.
- index.html is the Vite/bundler entry point that stitches together the viewer application — changes here affect the entire viewer build output contract.
- Logo images in source alongside HTML suggests these assets are co-located intentionally for the viewer build pipeline, not shared across packages.
- The HTML entry file and logo images have no automated contract test — build-output-contract.test.ts in web-integration tests the build output shape, which implicitly covers these, but a dedicated asset-presence assertion would make the contract explicit.
- Storing binary assets (PNG files) in src/ alongside source code is a common pattern for bundler-managed projects and is acceptable here; just ensure these are excluded from TypeScript compilation paths.
- This zone is cleanly isolated — zero cross-zone imports in or out — making it the safest zone in the codebase to modify without ripple effects.
- viewer-static-assets is the only zone in the entire web package with confirmed zero cross-zone coupling in both directions — every other zone participates in at least one import relationship, making viewer-static-assets uniquely invisible to import-graph drift detection; its primary guard must be build-output-contract assertions rather than import analysis.
- As the sole zero-coupling zone in the web package, viewer-static-assets sits completely outside the hub topology centered on web-viewer — architectural drift (e.g. an accidental JavaScript import added to index.html) would be undetected by any import-graph tooling and would only surface at build time.
- index.html has a manually maintained comment block documenting the no-CDN, all-local-assets policy — this is a good human-readable guard, but the policy has no automated enforcement: a developer could add an external <script src> tag that violates the policy without any test failing.
- The inline theme-detection script in index.html (lines 17-23) accesses localStorage directly — this script runs before the Preact application bootstraps and cannot be tested by the viewer unit suite, making it the only production JavaScript in the web package that is completely outside the test boundary.
- index.html's no-CDN policy is enforced only by a human-readable comment, not by an automated test — build-output-contract.test.ts could assert that no <script src> or <link href> tags in the emitted index.html point to external URLs, but currently does not, leaving the supply-chain security invariant unverifiable by CI.
- The inline theme-detection script in index.html runs before the Preact application mounts and accesses localStorage directly — it is the only production JavaScript in the web package with zero test coverage (neither unit tests nor build-output-contract assertions exercise it), making localStorage key name typos or theme-attribute name changes silently broken.
- viewer-static-assets is the only zone composed entirely of non-TypeScript, non-JavaScript files (one HTML file, two PNG binaries). Zone cohesion and coupling metrics are structurally inapplicable to this zone — not because it is 'isolated' in the architectural sense but because it has no import graph to measure. The 0/0 metric profile is an artifact of applying import-graph analysis to a pure-asset collection, not a meaningful structural signal.
- Zone detection assigns viewer-static-assets a 0/0 cohesion/coupling profile identical to an isolated code zone, but for a fundamentally different reason: no importable source exists. Tooling that uses cohesion/coupling metrics for zone health scoring will treat this zone as 'structurally equivalent to a self-contained module' when it is actually outside the import graph entirely. Tag this zone as type='assets' or exclude it from import-graph health scoring to prevent misleading equivalences in automated reports.
- Zone "logo" was identified from filename patterns, not directory structure — consider creating packages/web/src/viewer/logo/ and moving related files there

</insights>
